capabilities.name: Pi Plan Mode Extension
capabilities.summary: Plan lifecycle commands and persistence for Pi sessions.
notes: Workspace-local extension under .pi/extensions (not baked into Pi core).
Exact upstream/base origin should be confirmed and recorded in a follow-up pass.

capabilities.name: Pi Agent Memory Extension
capabilities.summary: Auto-injects memory/collaboration.md into agent system prompt via before_agent_start.

capabilities.name: Pi Appended Dev Rules
capabilities.summary: Always-on build instructions injected into Pi system prompt.
notes: Build standards consolidated into .pi/standards/build-standards.md.

capabilities.name: QMD Semantic Search
capabilities.summary: External CLI used for semantic/hybrid search with fallback behavior in core services.
notes: Can be available in workspace but underused; keep discoverable for agents.
When unavailable, core should gracefully fall back to token/keyword search.

capabilities.name: Google Calendar Provider
capabilities.summary: Cross-platform calendar integration via Google Calendar REST API with embedded OAuth client credentials and refresh-token persistence.
notes: OAuth callback uses localhost dynamic port and requires browser consent.
If Google shows an unverified-app warning, users continue via Advanced -> Go to Areté (unsafe).
JSON output (--json) includes: importance ('light'|'normal'|'important'), organizer ({name,email,self}|null), notes (string|null), hasAgenda (boolean)

capabilities.name: schedule-meeting
capabilities.description: Conversational skill for scheduling meetings and blocking focus time

capabilities.name: ship
capabilities.description: Mega-build skill automating plan-to-PR workflow: pre-mortem, review, memory scan, PRD creation, worktree setup, execute-prd, final wrap. 6 phases with intelligent gates.

capabilities.name: audit
capabilities.description: Orchestrate domain-expert subagents to audit and fix project documentation. Supports --scope for single-domain audit and --dry-run for preview mode.

capabilities.name: Commitments Service
capabilities.description: LLM-based commitment extraction, single source of truth for what is owed to/from people, bidirectional person-file sync, CLI list/resolve commands

capabilities.name: AI Service
capabilities.description: Unified AI integration wrapping pi-ai with task-based model routing, credential management, and structured output support. Provides callStructured() for typed JSON responses with TypeBox schema validation.
notes: Credentials stored in ~/.arete/credentials.yaml (API keys) and ~/.arete/auth.json (OAuth)
Uses testDeps injection pattern for mocking pi-ai calls in tests
Task routing: task -> tier -> model via config.ai
OAuth flow: browser consent -> paste code -> token auto-refresh

capabilities.name: Meeting Processing
capabilities.description: AI-powered meeting intelligence extraction and approval. Extracts summary, key points, decisions, action items, and next steps. CLI and backend use shared processing logic for full interchangeability.
notes: Requires AI configuration (arete credentials configure or arete.yaml)
extract --stage writes full metadata (status, confidence, source maps) for UI compatibility
extract --clear-approved clears approved sections before re-extracting
approve commits staged items to memory files (decisions.md, learnings.md)
processMeetingExtraction() in core used by both CLI and backend for consistency
Decisions and learnings default to 0.9 confidence when extracted

capabilities.name: Unified Workspace Search
capabilities.description: Consolidated search command replacing context --for, memory search, and memory timeline. Provides semantic search across all workspace areas with scope filtering, timeline mode, person filtering, and AI synthesis.
notes: Multi-collection QMD indexing: 6 scoped collections (all, memory, meetings, context, projects, people)
Deprecates: context --for, memory search, memory timeline (still work with warnings)
Person filter is post-QMD (--limit applies before filter)
Timeline mode uses MemoryService.getTimeline(), not QMD
--answer requires AI configuration

capabilities.name: Product Intelligence Dashboard
capabilities.description: Full web application for product intelligence visualization. Includes Dashboard, Meeting Triage (with detail view), People Intelligence (with detail view), Goals Alignment, Memory Feed, Intelligence Patterns, Commitments, Global Search, and Settings pages.
notes: Backend: Hono server with per-slug file locking
Web: React + Vite + TanStack Query v5 + TipTap editor
Launched via arete view command which spawns backend and opens browser
SSE endpoint for real-time meeting processing updates

capabilities.name: Daily Intelligence Brief
capabilities.description: Morning intelligence brief showing today's calendar, overdue commitments, active projects, recent decisions, and signal patterns.

capabilities.name: Momentum Tracking
capabilities.description: Commitment momentum (hot/stale/critical buckets) and relationship momentum (active/cooling/stale based on meeting recency). Pure computation functions.

capabilities.name: Signal Pattern Detection
capabilities.description: Cross-person signal pattern detection — identifies topics appearing in 2+ meetings across 2+ distinct people. Used for intelligence dashboard and daily brief.

capabilities.name: Context Service
capabilities.description: Assembles relevant workspace context for queries and skills. Maps product primitives to workspace files via PRIMITIVE_FILE_MAP. Detects coverage gaps. Provides context inventory with freshness tracking.
notes: Part of core service trio: Context + Memory + Entity
Injected into IntelligenceService for briefing assembly
Uses SearchProvider for semantic file matching

capabilities.name: Memory Service
capabilities.description: Manages workspace memory (decisions, learnings, observations). Provides search, creation, timeline building with theme extraction. Memory files: decisions.md, learnings.md, agent-observations.md in .arete/memory/items/.
notes: Part of core service trio: Context + Memory + Entity
Uses recency boosting for search relevance
Theme extraction requires minimum 3 occurrences

capabilities.name: Entity Service
capabilities.description: Resolves entity references (people, meetings, projects) via fuzzy matching. Manages people files (CRUD, list, memory refresh). Finds mentions and relationships across workspace. Includes People Intelligence for candidate triage.
notes: Part of core service trio: Context + Memory + Entity
SEARCH_PROVIDER_CANDIDATE_LIMIT = 100 triggers full scan fallback
Person Intelligence modules (person-*.ts) are implementation details, not exported from core index

capabilities.name: Intelligence Service
capabilities.description: Orchestrates Context, Memory, and Entity services to assemble primitive briefings. Routes queries to best skill via trigger matching, description overlap, and work type keywords. Prepares skill context with proactive search.
notes: Top-level orchestrator — never touches StorageAdapter directly
routeToSkill() uses stop words, trigger matching, description overlap, work type keywords
Service composition pattern: IntelligenceService(context, memory, entity)

capabilities.name: Apple Calendar Provider
capabilities.summary: macOS-only calendar integration via ical-buddy CLI. Default calendar provider for Mac users.
notes: Requires ical-buddy binary installed via Homebrew: brew install ical-buddy
macOS only — use google-calendar-provider for cross-platform
Provider alias: 'macos' → 'apple-calendar' in registry
JSON output (--json) includes: importance ('light'|'normal'|'important'), organizer ({name,email,self}|null), notes (string|null), hasAgenda (boolean)

capabilities.name: Krisp Integration
capabilities.summary: Meeting recording integration with AI summaries, transcripts, and action items via Krisp MCP API. OAuth PKCE authentication.
notes: Uses MCP (Model Context Protocol) over HTTP Streamable
OAuth requires browser consent flow
API uses get_multiple_documents (not get_document) for batch transcript fetch
SSE responses require Accept: application/json, text/event-stream header
Date filtering: add +1 day to 'before' to include today's meetings across timezones

capabilities.name: Fathom Integration
capabilities.summary: Meeting recording and transcription integration. Pulls summaries, transcripts, and action items from Fathom.
notes: API key from https://fathom.video/settings/api
Stored in FATHOM_API_KEY env var or .credentials/credentials.yaml
Follows same pattern as Krisp: thin fetch wrapper, not SDK

capabilities.name: Notion Integration
capabilities.summary: Documentation integration pulling Notion pages as markdown. Supports nested blocks, tables, code, images with rate limiting and dedup.
notes: API key from notion.so/profile/integrations (internal integration)
Stored in NOTION_API_KEY env var or .credentials/credentials.yaml
Thin fetch wrapper — no @notionhq/client SDK (567KB saved)
Rate limit: 3 req/sec with timestamp-based windowing
File URLs expire ~1 hour (S3 pre-signed URLs)
Supports 20+ block types; unknown types emit HTML comments

capabilities.name: Task Service
capabilities.description: GTD-style task management across now/week.md and now/tasks.md. Supports metadata tags (@area, @project, @person, @due, @from), cross-file CRUD, and auto-resolution of linked commitments.
notes: Task IDs are 8-char sha256 hash of normalized text (same pattern as CommitmentsService)
Bucket mapping: inbox/must/should/could → week.md, anytime/someday → tasks.md
Auto-resolution: completing task with @from(commitment:id) resolves linked commitment
Scoring service is stateless (pure functions)
Scoring dimensions: due date (0-40), commitment (0-25), meeting relevance (0-20), week priority (0-15)
